Work Experience

Where I've moved
the needle.

CRED
Campaign Operations Specialist
May 2024 – Present
Growth & Campaign Performance
  • Spearheaded end-to-end execution and monitoring of CRED Payback Milestones — owning full user journey from awareness to reward claim, tracking entry point CTRs, landing page conversions, and merchant-wise redemption across reward tiers (₹5K / ₹25K / ₹100K) — onboarding 850K+ users, sustaining 7+ sessions/user, and validating PMF for a new engagement surface.
  • Configured and monitored Mega Coin Rush (CR3) across 14.18M transacting users, 1.54M NTU activations, and 1.33M dormant user reactivations — driving 633B coins burned (+24.6%), 5.2M transacting users (+55.7%), and ₹17.48 Cr GMV (+170%).
  • Configured and launched 20+ category-specific campaigns across utility bills, rent, and education verticals — building personalized incentive models (cashback slabs, discount tiers) that drove +12% user activation and +10% reactivation.
Ops Efficiency & Tooling
  • Configured and shipped 100+ campaigns across CCBP and Wallet LOBs — managing eligibility rules, reward slabs, and DS cashback integration for NTU, ETU, and re-engagement segments — sustaining 4.3M+ MAU, 123B+ TPV, and ~96–97% allot rates with zero monetary leakage.
  • Reduced campaign configuration time by 66% (30 min → 10 min) by redesigning ops workflows in alignment with backend systems — improving accuracy and enabling faster iteration at scale.
  • Partnered with product and engineering to co-build improvements to internal campaign tooling (WIN, FALCON, ARF), reducing pre-launch ops overhead and improving reliability.
Analytics & Monitoring
  • Built and maintained campaign performance tracking across allocations, claims, reward utilization, and budget consumption using Mixpanel and Databricks — enabling real-time decisioning during live campaigns.
  • Coordinated with engineering to build an alert system for early detection of campaign exhaustion — tracking allocation and budget thresholds in real time to trigger proactive interventions before campaigns lost traction.
